schema.description: A retail e-commerce server providing user management, product catalog browsing, and order lifecycle operations including modifications, cancellations, returns, and exchanges.

tools.name: find_user_by_email
tools.description: Find user information by their registered email address.

email.description: The registered email address associated with the user account.

user_id.description: The unique identifier for the customer account.

name.description: The full name of the user.

email.description: The registered email address associated with the user account.

zip_code.description: The five-digit postal zip code of the user's address.

tools.name: find_user_by_name_zip
tools.description: Find user information by matching their legal name and zip code.

first_name.description: The legal first name of the user.

last_name.description: The legal last name of the user.

zip_code.description: The five-digit postal zip code of the user's address.

user_id.description: The unique identifier for the customer account.

name.description: The full name of the user.

email.description: The registered email address associated with the user account.

zip_code.description: The five-digit postal zip code of the user's address.

tools.name: get_user_details
tools.description: Retrieve comprehensive user profile including address, payment methods, and associated orders.

user_id.description: The unique identifier for the customer account.
The unique identifier for the customer account.

name.description: The user's name details, including first and last name components.

email.description: The registered email address associated with the user account.

address.description: The user's default shipping address details.

payment_methods.description: List of payment methods registered to the user account.

zip_code.description: The five-digit postal zip code of the user's address.

order_ids.description: List of order identifiers associated with the user account.

tools.name: modify_user_address
tools.description: Update the default shipping address for a user account.

user_id.description: The unique identifier for the customer account.

address.description: The primary line of the shipping address (e.g., street number and name).

city.description: The city name for the shipping address.

state.description: The state or province code for the shipping address.

country.description: The country name for the shipping address.

zip_code.description: The five-digit postal zip code for the shipping address.

user_id.description: The unique identifier for the customer account.

updated_address.description: The newly updated shipping address details.

tools.name: list_product_types
tools.description: List all available product categories and their basic information.

product_id.description: The unique identifier for the product type.

name.description: The display name of the product.

products.description: List of available product types with their identifiers and names.

tools.name: get_product_details
tools.description: Retrieve detailed information including description and available item variants for a product.

product_id.description: The unique identifier for the product type.

name.description: The display name of the product.

description.description: Detailed description of the product features and specifications.

tools.name: get_order_details
tools.description: Retrieve the status, items, and shipping details of a specific order.

order_id.description: The unique identifier for the order, including the '#' prefix (e.g., '#W0001').
The unique identifier for the order, including the '#' prefix.

user_id.description: The unique identifier for the customer account that placed the order.

status.description: The current status of the order (e.g., 'pending', 'delivered', 'cancelled').

item_ids.description: List of item identifiers in the order.

total_amount.description: The total monetary amount of the order.

address.description: The shipping address details for the order.

tools.name: modify_pending_order_address
tools.description: Change the shipping address for an order that is still in pending status.

order_id.description: The unique identifier for the order, including the '#' prefix (e.g., '#W0001').

address.description: The primary line of the shipping address (e.g., street number and name).

city.description: The city name for the shipping address.

state.description: The state or province code for the shipping address.

country.description: The country name for the shipping address.

zip_code.description: The five-digit postal zip code for the shipping address.

order_id.description: The unique identifier for the order, including the '#' prefix.

user_id.description: The unique identifier for the customer account that placed the order.

status.description: The current status of the order.

item_ids.description: List of item identifiers in the order.

total_amount.description: The total monetary amount of the order.

address.description: The updated shipping address details for the order.

tools.name: modify_pending_order_items
tools.description: Swap items in a pending order with other variants of the same product.

order_id.description: The unique identifier for the order, including the '#' prefix (e.g., '#W0001').

item_ids.description: List of current item identifiers to be removed from the order.

new_item_ids.description: List of new item identifiers to be added as replacements.

payment_method.description: The payment method used to settle any price difference resulting from the item swap.

order_id.description: The unique identifier for the order, including the '#' prefix.

user_id.description: The unique identifier for the customer account that placed the order.

status.description: The current status of the order.

items.description: The updated list of items in the order after modification.

total_amount.description: The updated total monetary amount of the order.

address.description: The shipping address details for the order.

tools.name: modify_pending_order_payment
tools.description: Update the payment method used for a pending order.

order_id.description: The unique identifier for the order, including the '#' prefix (e.g., '#W0001').

payment_method.description: The new payment method to be used for the order.

order_id.description: The unique identifier for the order, including the '#' prefix.

user_id.description: The unique identifier for the customer account that placed the order.

status.description: The current status of the order.

items.description: List of items in the order.

total_amount.description: The total monetary amount of the order.

address.description: The shipping address details for the order.

tools.name: cancel_pending_order
tools.description: Cancel a pending order with a specified cancellation reason.

order_id.description: The unique identifier for the order, including the '#' prefix (e.g., '#W0001').

reason.description: The valid reason for the cancellation request.

order_id.description: The unique identifier for the cancelled order, including the '#' prefix.

user_id.description: The unique identifier for the customer account that placed the order.

status.description: The updated status of the order (cancelled).

items.description: List of items that were in the cancelled order.

total_amount.description: The total monetary amount of the cancelled order.

address.description: The shipping address details for the order.

tools.name: return_delivered_order_items
tools.description: Request a return for specific items in a delivered order.

order_id.description: The unique identifier for the order, including the '#' prefix (e.g., '#W0001').

item_ids.description: List of item identifiers for items being returned.

payment_method.description: The payment method to receive the refund.

order_id.description: The unique identifier for the order, including the '#' prefix.

user_id.description: The unique identifier for the customer account that placed the order.

status.description: The updated status of the order (return requested).

items.description: List of items in the order with their return status.

total_amount.description: The total monetary amount of the order.

address.description: The shipping address details for the order.

tools.name: exchange_delivered_order_items
tools.description: Exchange delivered items for new variants of the same product.

order_id.description: The unique identifier for the order, including the '#' prefix (e.g., '#W0001').

item_ids.description: List of item identifiers for items to be exchanged.

new_item_ids.description: List of item identifiers for the new replacement items.

payment_method.description: The payment method used to settle any price difference resulting from the exchange.

order_id.description: The unique identifier for the order, including the '#' prefix.

user_id.description: The unique identifier for the customer account that placed the order.

status.description: The updated status of the order (exchange requested).

items.description: List of items in the order with their exchange status.

total_amount.description: The updated total monetary amount of the order after exchange.

address.description: The shipping address details for the order.

scenarios.description: Basic scenario with one user, one product, and one completed order

P001.name: Wireless Mouse
P001.description: Ergonomic wireless mouse with USB receiver

scenarios.description: Multiple users with various order statuses including returns and exchanges

P003.name: Desk Lamp
P003.description: LED desk lamp with adjustable brightness

scenarios.description: Rich scenario with multiple users, diverse products, and various order states including edge cases

P001.name: Mechanical Keyboard
P001.description: RGB mechanical gaming keyboard with blue switches

P005.name: Mouse Pad
P005.description: Extended gaming mouse pad with RGB lighting

scenarios.description: Boundary test with invalid email format and zip code pattern violations (should be rejected)

errors.error_details: KeyError: 'first_name' - The tool is trying to access a 'first_name' key that doesn't exist in the return value
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Tool should return user info with fields user_id, name, email, zip_code. Actual: Tool crashes with KeyError when trying to access 'first_name'
errors.root_cause: The api.find_user_by_email() method is likely returning a user object with nested name structure (name.first, name.last), but the tool wrapper is trying to flatten it incorrectly by accessing result['first_name'] instead of result['name']['first']

errors.error_details: KeyError: 'first_name' - The tool is trying to access a 'first_name' key that doesn't exist in the return value
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Tool should return user info with fields user_id, name, email, zip_code. Actual: Tool crashes with KeyError when trying to access 'first_name'
errors.root_cause: Same issue as find_user_by_email - the api.find_user_by_name_zip() method returns a user object with nested name structure, but the tool wrapper is trying to access result['first_name'] instead of result['name']['first']

tool_executions.note: Expected failure - order is delivered, not pending
Expected failure - order is delivered, not pending
Expected failure - order is delivered, not pending
Expected failure - order is delivered, not pending

save_scenario.notes: State was modified as expected: user address updated, order items exchanged

errors.error_details: KeyError: 'first_name' - The API method is trying to access user['name']['first_name'] but the correct key path is user['name']['first']
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Tool should return user info with user_id, name, email, zip_code. Actual: Tool raises KeyError trying to access 'first_name' key
errors.root_cause: The find_user_by_email API method is incorrectly accessing the name dictionary. The schema uses 'first' and 'last' keys, but the method is trying to access 'first_name' and 'last_name' keys. This is a bug in the API implementation.

errors.error_details: KeyError: 'first_name' - The API method is trying to access user['name']['first_name'] but the correct key path is user['name']['first']
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Tool should return user info when matching first_name, last_name, and zip_code. Actual: Tool raises KeyError trying to access 'first_name' key
errors.root_cause: The find_user_by_name_zip API method has the same bug as find_user_by_email - it's incorrectly accessing the name dictionary using 'first_name' and 'last_name' keys instead of 'first' and 'last' keys as defined in the schema.

errors.error_details: After modifying user address, the saved scenario shows inconsistent address structure. Original address has 'street' key, but modified address has 'address' key instead. Also includes 'zip_code' inside the address object.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Address structure should remain consistent with original schema {'street': ..., 'city': ..., 'state': ..., 'country': ...}. Actual: Modified address uses {'address': ..., 'city': ..., 'state': ..., 'country': ..., 'zip_code': ...}
errors.root_cause: The modify_user_address API method returns an inconsistent address structure. The 'updated_address' field uses 'address' key instead of 'street' key, and includes 'zip_code' inside the address object when it should be a separate field at the user level.

save_scenario.error: State inconsistency detected: User U001 address structure changed from {'street': '456 Oak Avenue', 'city': 'Portland', 'state': 'OR', 'country': 'USA'} to {'address': '123 New Street', 'city': 'Boston', 'state': 'MA', 'country': 'USA', 'zip_code': '02101'}. The address field structure is inconsistent - original uses 'street' key, modified uses 'address' key. Also, order #ORD001 status changed from 'delivered' to 'delivered' but item I002 status changed from 'normal' to 'returned'.

errors.error_details: KeyError: 'first_name' - The tool is trying to access user['name']['first_name'] but the API method is likely returning a different structure or encountering an error when building the response
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Should return user_id, name (full name string), email, zip_code. Actual: KeyError 'first_name' when trying to construct the response
errors.root_cause: The find_user_by_email API method is likely trying to format the name as a string (e.g., 'first_name last_name') but is accessing the name dictionary incorrectly. The user data has name stored as {'first': 'Emma', 'last': 'Williams'} but the code is trying to access 'first_name' instead of 'first'.

errors.error_details: KeyError: 'first_name' - The tool is trying to access user['name']['first_name'] but the user data structure uses 'first' and 'last' keys instead
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Should return user_id, name (full name string), email, zip_code. Actual: KeyError 'first_name' when trying to construct the response
errors.root_cause: Same as find_user_by_email - the API method is trying to access user['name']['first_name'] when it should be accessing user['name']['first']. This is a mismatch between the data schema and the API implementation.

errors.error_details: After modifying user U001's address, the saved scenario shows address structure with 'address' key instead of 'street' key. Original: {'street': '100 Market Street', ...}, Modified: {'address': '200 New Street', ...}
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Address structure should remain consistent with 'street' key. Actual: Address structure changed to use 'address' key instead of 'street'
errors.root_cause: The modify_user_address tool is storing the address with inconsistent field names. The tool parameter is named 'address' but should be stored as 'street' to match the original schema structure.

errors.error_details: After calling return_delivered_order_items on order #ORD001, the tool returned status 'return requested' but save_scenario shows status as 'delivered'
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Order status should be 'return requested' in saved scenario. Actual: Order status remains 'delivered' in saved scenario
errors.root_cause: The return_delivered_order_items tool returns the updated status in its response but doesn't persist the order status change to the internal state. Only the item status is updated to 'returned', but the order-level status change is not saved.

errors.error_details: After calling exchange_delivered_order_items on order #ORD003, the tool returned status 'exchange requested' but save_scenario shows status as 'delivered'
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Order status should be 'exchange requested' in saved scenario. Actual: Order status remains 'delivered' in saved scenario
errors.root_cause: Similar to return_delivered_order_items - the exchange_delivered_order_items tool returns the updated status in its response but doesn't persist the order status change to the internal state. The tool updates item statuses and adds new items but doesn't save the order-level status change.

tool_executions.error: Expected error: Order is not in pending status (order #ORD004 has status 'processing')
Expected error: Order is not in pending status (order #ORD004 has status 'processing')
Expected error: Order is not in pending status (order #ORD004 has status 'processing')
Expected error: Order is not in pending status (order #ORD004 has status 'processing')

save_scenario.error: State inconsistency detected: User U001 address structure mismatch. Original address has 'street' key, but saved address has 'address' key. Also, order #ORD001 status changed from 'delivered' to 'return requested' but saved as 'delivered'. Order #ORD003 status changed from 'delivered' to 'exchange requested' but saved as 'delivered'.

errors.error_details: Pydantic validation correctly rejected invalid email format 'invalid-email-format' (expected pattern: '^[\w\.-]+@[\w\.-]+\.\w+$') and invalid zip_code 'ABCDE' (expected pattern: '^\d{5}$')
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Validation error due to invalid data. Actual: Validation error occurred as expected with 2 validation errors for email and zip_code fields
errors.root_cause: This is a boundary test scenario designed to validate that the load_scenario tool properly enforces data validation rules. The scenario intentionally contains invalid data: (1) email 'invalid-email-format' does not match the required email pattern, and (2) zip_code 'ABCDE' contains letters instead of 5 digits. The Pydantic schema validation correctly caught both issues.

load_scenario.error:
2 validation errors for RetailScenario
users.U001.email
  String should match pattern '^[\w\.-]+@[\w\.-]+\.\w+$' [type=string_pattern_mismatch, input_value='invalid-email-format', input_type=str]
users.U001.zip_code
  String should match pattern '^\d{5}$' [type=string_pattern_mismatch, input_value='ABCDE', input_type=str]

save_scenario.error: Skipped due to load_scenario expected failure (validation_error scenario)

test_cases.actual: Correctly raised 'Order is not in pending status' error (order status is 'processing')

test_cases.actual: Correctly raised 'Order is not in pending status' error
test_cases.note: This is expected behavior - the tool correctly validates order status

test_cases.description: Error case - Attempt to modify non-pending order

test_cases.description: Valid case - Return item from delivered order
test_cases.expected: Item marked as returned, order status updated

test_cases.description: Error case - Attempt to exchange non-delivered order
test_cases.expected: Error: Order is not in delivered status
test_cases.actual: Correctly raised 'Order is not in delivered status' error (order status is 'shipped')
test_cases.note: This is expected behavior - the tool correctly validates order status

errors.error_details: After calling exchange_delivered_order_items on order #ORD003, the tool returned status 'exchange requested' but save_scenario shows status as 'delivered'
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Order status should be 'exchange requested' in saved scenario. Actual: Order status remains 'delivered' in saved scenario
errors.root_cause: Similar to return_delivered_order_items - the exchange_delivered_order_items tool returns the updated status in its response but doesn't persist the order status change to the internal state. The tool updates item statuses and adds new items but doesn't save the order-level status change.

save_scenario.error: State inconsistency detected: User U001 address structure mismatch. Original address has 'street' key, but saved address has 'address' key. Also, order #ORD001 status changed from 'delivered' to 'return requested' but saved as 'delivered'. Order #ORD003 status changed from 'delivered' to 'exchange requested' but saved as 'delivered'.

error.error_details: KeyError: 'first_name' - The tool is trying to access a 'first_name' key that doesn't exist in the return value
error.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Tool should return user info with fields user_id, name, email, zip_code. Actual: Tool crashes with KeyError when trying to access 'first_name'
error.root_cause: The api.find_user_by_email() method is likely returning a user object with nested name structure (name.first, name.last), but the tool wrapper is trying to flatten it incorrectly by accessing result['first_name'] instead of result['name']['first']

error.error_details: KeyError: 'first_name' - The API method is trying to access user['name']['first_name'] but the correct key path is user['name']['first']
error.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Tool should return user info with user_id, name, email, zip_code. Actual: Tool raises KeyError trying to access 'first_name' key
error.root_cause: The find_user_by_email API method is incorrectly accessing the name dictionary. The schema uses 'first' and 'last' keys, but the method is trying to access 'first_name' and 'last_name' keys. This is a bug in the API implementation.

error.error_details: KeyError: 'first_name' - The tool is trying to access user['name']['first_name'] but the API method is likely returning a different structure or encountering an error when building the response
error.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Should return user_id, name (full name string), email, zip_code. Actual: KeyError 'first_name' when trying to construct the response
error.root_cause: The find_user_by_email API method is likely trying to format the name as a string (e.g., 'first_name last_name') but is accessing the name dictionary incorrectly. The user data has name stored as {'first': 'Emma', 'last': 'Williams'} but the code is trying to access 'first_name' instead of 'first'.

error.error_details: KeyError: 'first_name' - The tool is trying to access a 'first_name' key that doesn't exist in the return value
error.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Tool should return user info with fields user_id, name, email, zip_code. Actual: Tool crashes with KeyError when trying to access 'first_name'
error.root_cause: Same issue as find_user_by_email - the api.find_user_by_name_zip() method returns a user object with nested name structure, but the tool wrapper is trying to access result['first_name'] instead of result['name']['first']

error.error_details: KeyError: 'first_name' - The API method is trying to access user['name']['first_name'] but the correct key path is user['name']['first']
error.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Tool should return user info when matching first_name, last_name, and zip_code. Actual: Tool raises KeyError trying to access 'first_name' key
error.root_cause: The find_user_by_name_zip API method has the same bug as find_user_by_email - it's incorrectly accessing the name dictionary using 'first_name' and 'last_name' keys instead of 'first' and 'last' keys as defined in the schema.

error.error_details: KeyError: 'first_name' - The tool is trying to access user['name']['first_name'] but the user data structure uses 'first' and 'last' keys instead
error.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Should return user_id, name (full name string), email, zip_code. Actual: KeyError 'first_name' when trying to construct the response
error.root_cause: Same as find_user_by_email - the API method is trying to access user['name']['first_name'] when it should be accessing user['name']['first']. This is a mismatch between the data schema and the API implementation.

error.error_details: After modifying user address, the saved scenario shows inconsistent address structure. Original address has 'street' key, but modified address has 'address' key instead. Also includes 'zip_code' inside the address object.
error.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Address structure should remain consistent with original schema {'street': ..., 'city': ..., 'state': ..., 'country': ...}. Actual: Modified address uses {'address': ..., 'city': ..., 'state': ..., 'country': ..., 'zip_code': ...}
error.root_cause: The modify_user_address API method returns an inconsistent address structure. The 'updated_address' field uses 'address' key instead of 'street' key, and includes 'zip_code' inside the address object when it should be a separate field at the user level.

error.error_details: After modifying user U001's address, the saved scenario shows address structure with 'address' key instead of 'street' key. Original: {'street': '100 Market Street', ...}, Modified: {'address': '200 New Street', ...}
error.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Address structure should remain consistent with 'street' key. Actual: Address structure changed to use 'address' key instead of 'street'
error.root_cause: The modify_user_address tool is storing the address with inconsistent field names. The tool parameter is named 'address' but should be stored as 'street' to match the original schema structure.

error.error_details: After calling return_delivered_order_items on order #ORD001, the tool returned status 'return requested' but save_scenario shows status as 'delivered'
error.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Order status should be 'return requested' in saved scenario. Actual: Order status remains 'delivered' in saved scenario
error.root_cause: The return_delivered_order_items tool returns the updated status in its response but doesn't persist the order status change to the internal state. Only the item status is updated to 'returned', but the order-level status change is not saved.

error.error_details: After calling exchange_delivered_order_items on order #ORD003, the tool returned status 'exchange requested' but save_scenario shows status as 'delivered'
error.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Order status should be 'exchange requested' in saved scenario. Actual: Order status remains 'delivered' in saved scenario
error.root_cause: Similar to return_delivered_order_items - the exchange_delivered_order_items tool returns the updated status in its response but doesn't persist the order status change to the internal state. The tool updates item statuses and adds new items but doesn't save the order-level status change.

test_cases.note: This is expected behavior - the tool correctly validates order status
